pois.name: Wartburg
pois.description: This UNESCO World Heritage site is where Martin Luther translated the New Testament into German. It is a premier example of Romanesque architecture and German history.

pois.name: Erfurter Dom
pois.description: The Cathedral of St. Mary features impressive Gothic architecture and the famous Gloriosa bell, the world's largest free-swinging medieval bell.

pois.name: Krämerbrücke
pois.description: A unique medieval structure, this is the longest inhabited bridge in Europe with buildings lining both sides across the Breitstrom river.

pois.name: Schloss Friedenstein
pois.description: One of Germany's best-preserved Early Baroque palace complexes, housing extensive art collections and the historic Ekhof Theatre.

pois.name: Gedenkstätte Buchenwald
pois.description: A solemn memorial site on the grounds of the former Nazi concentration camp, documenting the crimes of the regime and honoring its victims.

pois.name: Goethes Nationalmuseum
pois.description: The former residence of Johann Wolfgang von Goethe, where the famous poet lived for 50 years and composed many of his greatest works.

pois.name: Schloss Belvedere
pois.description: A Rococo pleasure palace south of Weimar, surrounded by extensive English-style landscape gardens and an orangery.

pois.name: Drachenschlucht
pois.description: A narrow gorge near Eisenach where hikers walk through rock walls only 68 centimeters apart at their narrowest point.

pois.name: Leuchtenburg
pois.description: Known as the 'Queen of the Saale Valley', this castle hosts a modern porcelain museum and offers expansive views over the landscape.

pois.name: Schloss Heidecksburg
pois.description: The former residence of the Princes of Schwarzburg-Rudolstadt, famous for its magnificent Rococo hall and miniature 'Rococo en miniature' exhibition.

pois.name: Kyffhäuser-Denkmal
pois.description: An enormous monument featuring Emperor Wilhelm I and Frederick Barbarossa, built atop the ruins of a much older imperial castle.

pois.name: Burg Hanstein
pois.description: One of the most impressive castle ruins in Central Germany, offering views of the tri-state border area between Thuringia, Hesse, and Lower Saxony.

pois.name: Schloss Burgk
pois.description: A picturesque castle complex on the Upper Saale, containing historic residential rooms and a notable chapel with a Silbermann organ.

pois.name: Dornburger Schlösser
pois.description: A group of three distinct palaces from different eras perched on a limestone cliff above the Saale valley.

pois.name: Saalfelder Feengrotten
pois.description: Former alum shale mines turned into colorful dripstone caves, officially listed as the most colorful grottoes in the world.

pois.name: Klosterruine Paulinzella
pois.description: One of the most important Romanesque monastery ruins in Germany, demonstrating the transition of architectural styles in the 12th century.

pois.name: Altstadt Mühlhausen
pois.description: A remarkably well-preserved medieval town center with a nearly complete city wall and numerous historic churches.

pois.name: Schloss Elisabethenburg
pois.description: The Baroque residence of the Dukes of Saxe-Meiningen, now housing extensive regional museum collections.

pois.name: Altstadt Schmalkalden
pois.description: Features a wealth of colorful half-timbered houses and historic squares associated with the Schmalkaldic League.

pois.name: Schloss Wilhelmsburg
pois.description: An exceptionally preserved Renaissance palace in Schmalkalden, virtually unchanged since its completion in the late 16th century.

pois.name: Point Alpha
pois.description: A former US Army observation post on the inner-German border, now a memorial illustrating the tensions of the Cold War.

pois.name: Hohenwarte-Stausee
pois.description: Known as the 'Thuringian Sea', this is part of the largest connected reservoir system in Germany, popular for water sports.

pois.name: Bleilochtalsperre
pois.description: The largest reservoir by volume in Germany, formed by damming the Saale river in the 1930s.

pois.name: Trusetaler Wasserfall
pois.description: An artificial waterfall created by diverting water from the Truse river, falling 58 meters over several rock steps.

pois.name: Baumkronenpfad Hainich
pois.description: A high-altitude canopy walk through the beech forests of Hainich National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage natural site.

pois.name: Funkenburg Westgreußen
pois.description: A reconstructed Germanic fortified settlement based on archaeological excavations of an Iron Age site.

pois.name: Alte Synagoge Erfurt
pois.description: The oldest synagogue building preserved up to its roof in Central Europe, housing the spectacular Erfurt Treasure.

pois.name: Marienkirche Mühlhausen
pois.description: A five-aisled Gothic hall church where Johann Sebastian Bach worked as an organist early in his career.

pois.name: Residenzschloss Altenburg
pois.description: Perched on a porphyry rock, this castle was the seat of the Dukes of Saxe-Altenburg and features a notable historic organ.

pois.name: Kloster Volkenroda
pois.description: A former Cistercian monastery featuring a modern 'Christus-Pavillon' from Expo 2000 integrated into the historic site.

pois.name: Hülfensberg
pois.description: The most important pilgrimage site in the Eichsfeld region, featuring a medieval cross and a Franciscan monastery.

pois.name: Werrabrücke Vacha
pois.description: A historic stone arch bridge across the Werra that served as a symbol of German division during the Cold War.

pois.name: Großer Inselsberg
pois.description: One of the highest and most prominent peaks in the Thuringian Forest, providing 360-degree views of the surrounding regions.

pois.name: Steinerne Rose
pois.description: A natural rock formation near Saalburg-Ebersdorf that resembles a blooming rose due to volcanic activity and erosion.

pois.name: Veste Heldburg
pois.description: Home to the German Castle Museum, this fortress offers insights into the history and life within German defensive structures.

pois.name: Burg Ranis
pois.description: A majestic castle sitting on a high cliff, known for the prehistoric Ilsenhöhle cave located directly beneath its walls.

pois.name: Schloss Molsdorf
pois.description: Often called the 'Versailles of Thuringia', this late Baroque palace is known for its elegant interiors and park.

pois.name: Köstritzer Schwarzbierbrauerei
pois.description: One of Germany's oldest producers of black beer, with brewing traditions documented back nearly five centuries.

pois.name: Oberweißbacher Bergbahn
pois.description: A unique broad-gauge funicular railway connecting Obstfelderschmiede and Cursdorf in the Thuringian Highlands.

pois.name: Gedenkstätte Andreasstraße
pois.description: A former Stasi prison in Erfurt that now serves as a museum dedicated to repression and resistance in the GDR.

pois.name: Lutherhaus Eisenach
pois.description: One of the oldest timber-framed buildings in Thuringia, where Martin Luther lived as a student with the Cotta family.

pois.name: Bachhaus Eisenach
pois.description: The first museum in the world dedicated to Johann Sebastian Bach, located in the town where the composer was born.

pois.name: Kickelhahn
pois.description: A mountain near Ilmenau famous for the Goethehäuschen, where Goethe wrote his famous poem 'Wanderer's Night Song'.

pois.name: Burg Gleichen
pois.description: One of the 'Three Equals' (Drei Gleichen) castles, this ruin is famous for the legend of the Count von Gleichen and his two wives.

pois.name: Mühlburg
pois.description: The oldest of the three Gleichen castles, offering a view of the Thuringian Basin from its preserved keep.

pois.name: Veste Wachsenburg
pois.description: The only fully restored castle of the 'Three Equals' group, today serving as a hotel, restaurant, and museum.

pois.name: Schlosspark Tiefurt
pois.description: A modest palace that served as the summer residence of Anna Amalia and was a center of Weimar's cultural life.

pois.name: JenTower
pois.description: A 144-meter skyscraper in Jena, originally designed for the Zeiss company, offering the best panoramic view of the Saale valley.

pois.name: Park an der Ilm
pois.description: A classic landscape garden in Weimar created in the late 18th century with significant input from Goethe.

pois.name: Herzogin Anna Amalia Bibliothek
pois.description: A historic library housing rare collections and the famous Rococo Hall, which was restored after a major fire in 2004.

pois.name: Gera Marktplatz
pois.description: The central square of Gera, featuring a Renaissance town hall and the Simson fountain.

pois.name: Residenzschloss Sondershausen
pois.description: The former seat of the Princes of Schwarzburg-Sondershausen, notable for its diverse architectural styles from Renaissance to Classicism.

pois.name: Panorama Museum Bad Frankenhausen
pois.description: Houses Werner Tübke's monumental painting of the Early Bourgeois Revolution in Germany, one of the largest oil paintings in the world.

pois.name: Schloss Kochberg
pois.description: The former estate of the von Stein family, famously visited by Goethe during his frequent travels to the region.

pois.name: Oberes Schloss Greiz
pois.description: A majestic castle perched on a hill above Greiz, former residence of the Princes Reuss of the Elder Line.

pois.name: Unteres Schloss Greiz
pois.description: Located in the valley by the Elster River, this palace showcases Neo-Gothic architecture and local history.

pois.name: Klosterruine Veilsdorf
pois.description: Remnants of a Benedictine monastery that played a role in the regional development of the Werra valley.

pois.name: Landgrafenschlucht
pois.description: A natural canyon near Eisenach featuring mossy rock walls and unique geological formations, adjacent to the Dragon's Gorge.

pois.name: Spitterfall
pois.description: The highest natural waterfall in Thuringia, located on the Spitter stream within the Thuringian Forest near Tambach-Dietharz.

pois.name: Grenzlandmuseum Eichsfeld
pois.description: Located at the former border crossing Duderstadt/Worbis, this museum documents the history of German division in the Eichsfeld.
